description: Distinguish pure JVM Kotlin from Android source sets and lifecycle constraints.
body: Use when a repo has Android Gradle Plugin, androidMain/androidTest, or app/library modules. Do not import Android APIs into common JVM modules or assume plain JUnit covers lifecycle behavior. Inspect source sets and Gradle plugins, then pick unit, instrumented, or Robolectric-style validation based on existing setup. Risk boundary includes SDK availability and device/emulator requirements. Use when Kotlin Engineering work touches repository conventions, toolchain files, changed symbols, validation commands, and runtime output. Do not use this guidance to override a more specific pack, user instruction, or repository contract. Actions: choose the smallest direction that preserves the domain contract; inspect the current files first, keep the change scoped to the owning boundary, and record what evidence changed the decision. Validation signals include package-local checks, focused tests, build output, smoke runs, and command logs. Risk boundary: escalate or stop when the task involves broad rewrites, generated-file drift, stale assumptions, and unvalidated behavior changes, missing owner evidence, or live external state.
